item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As always, Prometheus Labs comes up with a really interesting piece of tech that could possibly change the world and… immediately use it for the most half assed and unadvisable thing they could think of, another proof of their Cartoon villain logic. This cliché really rustles my jimmies.

However, this skip is interesting and the testings are thorough. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not really feeling these. First one is okay, maybe. Next two don't make sense as internal hyperlinks as they're from an actual PL document - not something the Foundation would have edited and added remarks to, unless it directly related to the SCP in question.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Edit: First Edit Made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show proposed edits
- Hide

In order to link to the mentioned GOIS, the following edits are proposed:

Instances of SCP-2325-1 superficially appear to be standard security headsets, outside of a small label with consecutive serial number and the marking 'Prometheus Labs' in relief on the back of the main unit.

to

Instances of SCP-2325-1 superficially appear to be standard security headsets, outside of a small label with consecutive serial number and the marking 'Prometheus Labs' in relief on the back of the main unit.

and

Personal, short-distance relativistic travel from [REDACTED] and the workable, albeit cryptic and incomplete information on psychic 'radar' recovered from GRU Division "P",

to

Personal, short-distance relativistic travel from [REDACTED] and the workable, albeit cryptic and incomplete information on psychic 'radar' recovered from GRU Division "P",

and

Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd would certainly be interested

to

Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd would certainly be interested

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, my interpretation is that this suggests Prometheus Labs has something more "safe" in this application. Which should be unsurprising because anartists/wizards alway were portrayed as stupidly hard to kill. The reason that the caps necessitate a "Get down, Mr. President!" situation is merely for the purpose of plausible deniability. It would not look good if the VIP suddenly teleported out of the way of the bullet, or a shield suddenly materialized out of thin-air. Of course, it'd be messy too if the person with the cap was separated from the VIP, but such is the cost of giving magic toys to rich idiots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-11000's accelerometer exceeds 4000m/s

This needs to be 4000 m/s2.

Otherwise +1 very fun to read the experiment logs and Test D-3 had me making a funny noise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you really to get into it, you don't even have to have a secondary ANYTHING. They can just teleport out of the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Test 2325-A-7:

The slug liquefies D-2776, coating 18 sq meters, the viewing window, and D-2776 with organic material.

Presumably D-1800 is the one coated with organic material, given the context. I feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On a similar note: what happens if two or more 2325-1 units are available when the VIP is shot at? Is there a decision mechanism, or do they all activate? What the hell would multiple units activating for the same bullet look like?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for fridge horror: given the capabilities of the system, the designers could've gotten the same effect without attaching 2325-1 to a living creature. Hell, that probably would've been easier- why go to the trouble of increasing the durability of a human shield when you could just use a block of depleted uranium instead?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's one test that hasn't been done that I want to see done.
What happens if D-XXXX (wearing 2325-1) and D-YYYY (wearing 2325-2) are positioned in such a way that, in order to intercept a bullet fired at D-YYYY, the most direct movement path would teleport D-XXXX _through_ D-YYYY?
That is to say, put D-YYYY between D-XXXX and the gun, and shoot at D-YYYY. Will D-XXXX teleport through D-YYYY safely? Or will he teleport through D-YYYY and kill D-YYYY in the process of teleporting through him, as evidenced in the trial in which someone else teleports through another D-class?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It would be interesting to see if some more out of the way projectiles would trigger the effect, e.g. throwing knives, lethally poisonous throwing darts, a lethal dose of tranquillisers (or a lethal dose of anything, really) via tranquilliser gun… Maybe a syringe filled with a substance that would kill in long term (e.g. something radioactive, or slow-acting poison).

Anyway, I really liked this idea. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the B series of tests, if you look at the parameters on B-1, the AM rifle is replaced with a service rifle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-Class are used as test subjects, both for lethal and nonlethal testing.

If you're testing the limits of a SCP, especially one where a human is required to activate it, you're going to end up performing tests that would absolutely be lethal to a normal human.

An Anti-Material rifle has a higher muzzle velocity than a service pistol or rifle, as well as being almost automatically lethal if it hits a person. Both factors may impact how the SCP functions when worn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the B series of tests, why would an anti-materiel rifle be necessary as the weapon? Couldn't they just use the service pistol again, seeing as everybody survived that one? D-class are expensive.

(I get that they died anyway, but it still seems like kind of a waste seeing that they didn't know what the results would be for B-1.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am far, far too amused by this one.

Normally I might agree with TyGently about the number of experiment logs, but I love the slow build here as they keep getting more and more fucked-up results.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My mistake - I knew I mixed up D-class somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Parameters: D-2289, accompanied by Agent Morgan, will enter SCP-2122. In an adjacent area at Site-48, a mechanical device will shoot D-7887.

D-6227 hit with bullet.

??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to see a test with a laser.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love scips with a good batch of in-depth testing logs. <3
Really dig the thorough testing here, digging into the mechanics and limitations of these things- excellent use of "show, don't tell".
Enthusiastic +1, and hope to see more from this author in the future

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea and PL connection are great stuff, but it always rubs me the wrong way when you have to read through a huge slog of experiment logs that don't really add much. Most of them just state things we already could have inferred, or are already outright stated in the article. Novote.

item.title: Re: Novoting for now
item.content:encoded: As I stated earlier I think I had a fundamental misunderstanding when writing the business case - funny that no one mentioned it in the drafting/critique phase, but I'll either swap the format to an internal document (therefore less formal) or rewrite it more formally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I'll have those fixed in a couple moments. I think I had a bit of a misunderstanding with the business case - my intention was to have it sound like a casual internal suggestion for a possible product that he ended up running with in the worst possible direction, rather than a formal business case.

item.title: Novoting for now
item.content:encoded: The concept and execution are great stuff, and I'd expect this kind of product from Prometheus Labs - but the PL format is too colloquial (especially its gratuitous usage of 1st person pronouns) and comes off more as a draft for a sales pitch than a grant request.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few typos:

two fiber optic lines, who construction

SCP-2325 system will also no function

could be used to protected our own personnel

anti material cartridges

The idea is rather terrific. I'm not wholly sold on the execution - the PL format in particular felt a bit simplistic for their usual tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is neat.
